retry_reason: Failure occurs only on memory allocation failure; retrying immediately without freeing memory would likely also fail, so retry is pointless.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for the global hist_pads percpu array. If the array already exists (refcount non-zero), increments the refcount and returns 0. If not, allocates the percpu array, sets refcount to 1, and returns 0. On allocation failure, returns -ENOMEM. The caller must hold event_mutex and must pair each successful call with a corresponding decrement (put) when done using the array. The caller must check the return value before using the array.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference on the global hist_pads percpu array, either by incrementing an existing refcount or by allocating and initializing it with refcount 1. The only non-refcount logic is the percpu allocation itself, which is intrinsic to the get-or-create pattern.
judge.retry_reason: Failure occurs only on memory allocation failure; retrying immediately without freeing memory would likely also fail, so retry is pointless.

check.reason: Both operations are consistent with the code: refcount_inc is called on hist_pad_ref when its value is positive, and refcount_set is called when it is zero after allocation. The global variable hist_pad_ref is of type refcount_t, its member refs is atomic_t, matching the callee's path r.refs and type chain refcount_t--atomic_t. The access path has 2 segments matching 2 types.
